codeReview.summary: The implementation creates a comprehensive backfill verification script (scripts/verify_backfill.py, 515 lines) and thorough test suite (tests/test_verify_backfill.py, 321 lines). The verification script covers all four required checks: stock kline counts, index kline counts, OHLCV validity, and gap detection against trade_calendar. Tests verify scheduler compatibility (INDEX_LIST has 8 indices, StockUpdater reads from watchlist dynamically, no hardcoded stock lists), SQLite WAL mode, and quant-dashboard MarketReader integration. The code follows existing patterns well. Two non-blocking issues noted below.

issues.description: log_to_database() writes status as uppercase string 'COMPLETED'/'FAILED' via raw sqlite3, but the DataUpdateStatus enum values are lowercase ('completed'/'failed'). While this doesn't cause a runtime crash (raw SQL insert bypasses SQLAlchemy enum validation), it creates inconsistent data in the data_update_log table — all other entries use lowercase values via SQLAlchemy. Should use 'completed'/'failed' to match the enum.

OHLCV NULL check only checks open/high/low/close but not volume. The feature description says 'validates OHLCV no NULLs' — the V (volume) is omitted from the NULL check query. However, the klines table schema defines volume as NOT NULL, so this is a minor gap rather than a functional bug.

The script uses raw sqlite3 directly instead of the project's SQLAlchemy database layer. While this works and avoids session management complexity for a standalone script, it bypasses the WAL mode pragma event listener in database.py (the raw connection won't get PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL set automatically). The log_to_database insert will still work since WAL mode is connection-level and the main db was already set to WAL, but it's an inconsistency with the project's data access patterns.

test_market_reader_reads_stock_klines hardcodes the absolute path '/Users/wendy/work/trading-co/ashare/data/market.db'. While this matches the production path and the test skips if the file doesn't exist, it makes the test inherently non-portable. This is consistent with MarketReader's own DEFAULT_DB_PATH though, so it's a pre-existing pattern.

codeQualityIssues.description: log_to_database() writes status as uppercase 'COMPLETED'/'FAILED' but DataUpdateStatus enum uses lowercase 'completed'/'failed', creating inconsistent data in data_update_log table.
OHLCV NULL check omits volume (the V in OHLCV). The klines schema has volume NOT NULL so this is a minor gap, not a functional bug.
Script uses raw sqlite3 instead of SQLAlchemy, bypassing the project's database layer and WAL mode pragma event listener.
Test hardcodes absolute path to market.db, though this is consistent with the existing MarketReader pattern.

sharedStateObservations.description: The environment.md file lists only 5 indices (上证指数, 深证成指, 创业板指, 科创50, 北证50) but the system now has 8 indices after the historical-backfill milestone added 沪深300, 中证500, and 中证1000. The data source documentation in environment.md is outdated.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: environment.md says '5 indices: 上证指数(000001.SH), 深证成指(399001.SZ), 创业板指(399006.SZ), 科创50(000688.SH), 北证50(899050.BJ)' but INDEX_LIST in src/services/index_updater.py now has 8 entries including 000300.SH, 000905.SH, 000852.SH.

sharedStateObservations.description: The environment.md lists stock count as '422 A-share stocks' and data range as '~2025-11-03 to 2026-03-24', but after the historical-backfill milestone the stock universe expanded to ~1000 stocks and data range extends back to ~2021. These figures are stale.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: environment.md says '422 A-share stocks with daily K-lines' and 'Data range: ~2025-11-03 to 2026-03-24'. The backfill feature description says the watchlist now has ~1000 stocks and data goes back 5 years.

sharedStateObservations.description: The architecture.md still refers to '422 stocks + 5 indices' in the system architecture diagram. Should be updated to reflect the expanded universe.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md system diagram shows '422 stocks + 5 indices' but the system now has ~1000 stocks and 8 indices after historical-backfill.

summary: Pass. The implementation comprehensively covers all four required verification areas: stock kline count checks, index kline count checks with all 8 indices, OHLCV validity (no NULLs, high >= low), and gap detection against trade_calendar. Tests thoroughly verify scheduler compatibility — confirming INDEX_LIST has 8 indices, StockUpdater reads from watchlist table dynamically with no hardcoded lists, and SQLite WAL mode is enabled. The quant-dashboard MarketReader integration is also tested. Four non-blocking issues identified: status enum case mismatch in log_to_database, volume omitted from OHLCV NULL check, use of raw sqlite3 instead of SQLAlchemy, and hardcoded absolute path in test. None are blocking — the script functions correctly for its verification purpose. Three shared state observations: environment.md and architecture.md still reference pre-backfill figures (5 indices / 422 stocks) instead of the current 8 indices / ~1000 stocks.
